children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There is established within the National Institutes of Health the Advanced Research Projects Agency–Health (referred to in this section as “ARPA–H”). Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall transfer all functions, personnel, missions, activities, authorities, and funds of the Advanced Research Projects Agency for Health as in existence on , to ARPA–H established by the preceding sentence.December 29, 2022December 29, 2022

children.label: i
children.text: an Office of the Director;

children.label: ii
children.text: not more than 8 program offices; and

children.label: iii
children.text: such special project offices as the Director may establish.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: B Requirement
children.text: Not fewer than two-thirds of the program offices of ARPA–H shall be exclusively dedicated to supporting research and development activities, consistent with the goals and functions described in subsection (b).

children.heading: Notification
children.label: C Notification
children.text: The Director shall submit a notification to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives if the Director determines that additional program offices are required to carry out this section.

children.heading: Exemption from certain policies of NIH
children.label: 3 Exemption from certain policies of NIH

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided for in this section, and subject to subparagraph (B), in establishing ARPA–H pursuant to paragraph (1), the Secretary may exempt ARPA–H from policies and requirements of the National Institutes of Health that are in effect on the day before , as necessary and appropriate to ensure ARPA–H can most effectively achieve the goals described in subsection (b)(1).December 29, 2022

children.heading: Notice
children.label: B Notice
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , the Secretary shall publish a notice in the Federal Register describing the specific policies and requirements of the National Institutes of Health from which the Secretary intends to exempt ARPA–H, including a rationale for such exemptions.December 29, 2022

children.label: A
children.text: foster the development of novel, breakthrough, and broadly applicable capabilities and technologies to accelerate transformative innovation in biomedical science and medicine in a manner that cannot be readily accomplished through traditional Federal biomedical research and development programs or commercial activity;

children.label: B
children.text: revolutionize the detection, diagnosis, mitigation, prevention, treatment, and cure of diseases and health conditions by overcoming long-term and significant technological and scientific barriers to developing transformative health technologies;

children.label: C
children.text: promote high-risk, high-reward innovation to enable the advancement of transformative health technologies; and

children.label: i
children.text: pursues initiatives that aim to maintain global leadership in science and innovation; and

children.label: ii
children.text: improves the health and wellbeing of its citizens by supporting the advancement of biomedical science and innovation.

children.label: A
children.text: discovery, identification, and promotion of revolutionary advancements in science;

children.label: B
children.text: translation of scientific discoveries into transformative health technologies with potential application for biomedical science and medicine;

children.label: C
children.text: creation of platform capabilities that draw on multiple disciplines;

children.label: D
children.text: delivery of proofs of concept that demonstrate meaningful advances with potential clinical application;

children.label: E
children.text: development of new capabilities and methods to identify potential targets and technological strategies for early disease detection and intervention, such as advanced computational tools and predictive models; and

children.label: F
children.text: acceleration of transformational health technological advances in areas with limited technical certainty.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The President shall appoint a director of ARPA–H (in this section referred to as the “Director”).

children.label: i
children.text: research and development programs; and

children.label: ii
children.text: large-scale, high-risk initiatives with respect to health research and technology development across multiple sectors, including identifying and supporting potentially transformative health technologies; and

children.label: B
children.text: has a demonstrated ability to identify and develop partnerships to address strategic needs in meeting the goals described in subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: Reporting
children.label: 3 Reporting
children.text: The Director shall report to the Secretary of Health and Human Services.

children.label: A
children.text: Establish strategic goals, objectives, and priorities for ARPA–H to advance the goals described in subsection (b)(1).

children.label: B
children.text: Approve the projects and programs of ARPA–H and restructure, expand, or terminate any project or program within ARPA–H that is not achieving its goals.

children.label: C
children.text: Develop funding criteria and assess the success of programs through the establishment of technical milestones.

children.label: D
children.text: Request that applications for funding disclose current and previous research and development efforts related to such applications, as appropriate, and identify any challenges associated with such efforts, including any scientific or technical barriers encountered in the course of such efforts or challenges in securing sources of funding, as applicable.

children.label: E
children.text: Coordinate with the heads of relevant Federal departments and agencies to facilitate sharing of data and information, as applicable and appropriate, and ensure that research supported by ARPA–H is informed by and supplements, not supplants, the activities of such departments and agencies and is free of unnecessary duplication of effort.

children.label: F
children.text: Ensure ARPA–H does not provide funding for a project unless the program manager determines that the project aligns with the goals described in subsection (b)(1).

children.label: i
children.text: scientific opportunity and potential impact, especially in areas that fit within the strategies and operating practices of ARPA–H and require public-private partnerships to effectively advance research and development activities; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the potential applications that an innovation may have to address areas of currently unmet need in medicine and health, including health disparities and the potential to prevent progression to serious disease.

children.label: H
children.text: Encourage strategic collaboration and partnerships with a broad range of entities, which may include institutions of higher education, minority-serving institutions (defined, for the purposes of this section, as institutions and programs described in and institutions described in ), industry, nonprofit organizations, Federally funded research and development centers, or consortia of such entities.section 1063b(e)(1) of title 20section 1067q(a) of title 20

children.label: A
children.text: shall be appointed for a 4-year term; and

children.label: B
children.text: may be reappointed for 1 consecutive 4-year term.

children.heading: Autonomy of Agency regarding recommendations and testimony
children.label: 6 Autonomy of Agency regarding recommendations and testimony
children.text: No office or agency of the United States shall have authority to require the Director to submit legislative recommendations, or testimony or comments on legislation, to any officer or agency of the United States for approval, comments, or review prior to the submission of such recommendations, testimony or comments to Congress, if such recommendations, testimony, or comments to Congress include a statement indicating that the views expressed therein are those of the Director and do not necessarily reflect the views of the President or another Federal department, agency, or office.

children.heading: Deputy Director
children.label: 7 Deputy Director
children.text: The Director shall appoint a Deputy Director to serve as the principal assistant to the Director.

children.heading: Nonapplication of certain provision
children.label: 8 Nonapplication of certain provision
children.text: The restrictions contained in section 202 of the Departments of Labor, Health and Human Services, and Education, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 1993 (; note) related to consultants and individual scientists appointed for limited periods of time shall not apply to the Director appointed under this subsection.Public Law 102–39442 U.S.C. 238f

children.heading: Application of certain flexibilities
children.label: d Application of certain flexibilities
children.text: The flexibilities provided to the National Institutes of Health under shall apply to ARPA–H with respect to the functions described in subsection (b)(2).section 241(g) of this title

children.heading: No authorization for disclosure
children.label: 1 No authorization for disclosure
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed as authorizing the Director to disclose any information that is a trade secret or other privileged or confidential information subject to or .section 552(b)(4) of title 5section 1905 of title 18

children.label: A
children.text: the number of instances in which the Secretary has used the authority under this subsection to withhold information from disclosure; and

children.label: B
children.text: the nature of any request under or that was denied using such authority.section 552 of title 5section 1905 of title 18

children.heading: Clarification
children.label: 3 Clarification
children.text: The protections for trade secrets or other privileged or confidential information described in paragraph (1) shall not be construed to limit the availability or disclosure of information necessary to inform and facilitate the evaluation required under subsection (k)(2). Any such information made available to members of the National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine (referred to in this section as the “National Academies”) for such evaluation shall be kept confidential by such members and shall not be used for any purposes other than informing and facilitating the evaluation required under subsection (k)(2).

children.heading: Cooperation with the Food and Drug Administration
children.label: f Cooperation with the Food and Drug Administration

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In order to facilitate the enhanced collaboration and communication with respect to the most current priorities of ARPA–H, the Food and Drug Administration may meet with ARPA–H and any other Federal partners at appropriate intervals to discuss the development status, and actions that may be taken to facilitate the development, of medical products and projects that are the highest priorities to ARPA–H.

children.heading: Reimbursement
children.label: 2 Reimbursement
children.text: Utilizing interagency agreements or other appropriate resource allocation mechanisms available, the Director shall reimburse, using funds made available to ARPA–H, the Food and Drug Administration, as appropriate, for activities identified by the Commissioner of Food and Drugs and the Director as being conducted by the Food and Drug Administration under the authority of this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: award grants and cooperative agreements, which shall include requirements to publicly report indirect facilities and administrative costs, broken out by fixed capital costs, administrative overhead, and labor costs;

children.label: B
children.text: award contracts, which may include multi-year contracts subject to ;section 3903 of title 41

children.label: C
children.text: award cash prizes, utilizing the authorities and processes established under ; andsection 3719 of title 15

children.label: D
children.text: enter into other transactions, as defined by , subject to paragraph (2).section 247d–7e(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: Limitations on entering into other transactions
children.label: 2 Limitations on entering into other transactions

children.heading: Use of competitive procedures
children.label: A Use of competitive procedures
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, competitive procedures shall be used when entering into other transactions under this section.

children.label: i
children.text: submits a request to the Director for each individual use of such authority before conducting or supporting a program, including an explanation of why the use of such authority is essential to promoting the success of the project;

children.label: ii
children.text: receives approval for the use of such authority from the Director; and

children.label: iii
children.text: for each year in which the program manager has used such authority in accordance with this paragraph, submits a report to the Director on the activities of the program related to such project.

children.heading: Exemptions from certain requirements
children.label: 3 Exemptions from certain requirements
children.text: Research funded by ARPA–H shall not be subject to the requirements of or .section 284a(a)(3)(A)(ii) of this titlesection 289a of this title

children.label: A
children.text: acquire (by purchase, lease, condemnation or otherwise), construct, improve, repair, operate, and maintain such real and personal property as are necessary to carry out this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: lease an interest in property for not more than 20 years, notwithstanding .section 1341(a)(1) of title 31

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: ARPA–H, including its headquarters, shall not be located on any part of the existing National Institutes of Health campuses.

children.heading: Number of locations
children.label: B Number of locations
children.text: ARPA–H shall have offices or facilities in not less than 3 geographic areas.

children.label: i
children.text: the characteristics of the intended location; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the extent to which such location will facilitate advancement of the goals and functions specified in subsection (b).

children.label: A
children.text: appoint and remove scientific, engineering, medical, and professional personnel, which may include temporary or term-limited appointments as determined by the Director to fulfill the mission of ARPA–H, without regard to any provision in title 5 governing appointments and removals under the civil service laws;

children.label: B
children.text: notwithstanding any other provision of law, including any requirement with respect to General Schedule pay rates under subchapter III of chapter 53 of title 5, fix the base pay compensation of such personnel at a rate to be determined by the Director, up to the amount of annual compensation (excluding expenses) specified in ; andsection 102 of title 3

children.label: C
children.text: contract with private recruiting firms for assistance in identifying highly qualified candidates for technical positions needed to carry out this section.

children.heading: Support staff
children.label: 2 Support staff
children.text: The Director may use authorities in existence on , that are provided to the Secretary to hire administrative, financial, clerical, and other staff necessary to carry out functions that support the goals and functions described in subsection (b).December 29, 2022

children.heading: Number of personnel
children.label: 3 Number of personnel
children.text: The Director may appoint not more than 210 personnel under this section. The Director shall submit a notification to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives if the Director determines that additional personnel are required to carry out this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the Director shall ensure that the personnel who are appointed to staff or support ARPA–H are individuals who, at the time of appointment and for 3 years prior to such appointment, were not employed by the National Institutes of Health. The Director may grant an exemption only for individuals who are uniquely qualified, by way of professional background and expertise, to advance the goals and functions specified in subsection (b).

children.heading: Nonapplication of provision
children.label: B Nonapplication of provision
children.text: The restriction provided under subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any individuals who are employed by ARPA–H on .December 29, 2022

children.label: A
children.text: may contract with private entities for the purposes of recruitment services;

children.label: B
children.text: shall make efforts to recruit a diverse workforce, including individuals underrepresented in science, engineering, and medicine, including racial and ethnic minorities, provided such efforts do not conflict with applicable Federal civil rights law, and individuals with a variety of professional experiences or backgrounds; and

children.label: C
children.text: shall recruit program managers with demonstrated expertise in a wide range of scientific disciplines and management skills.

children.heading: Use of Intergovernmental Personnel Act
children.label: 6 Use of Intergovernmental Personnel Act
children.text: To the extent needed to carry out the authorities under paragraph (1) and the goals and functions specified in subsection (b), the Director may utilize hiring authorities under sections 3371 through 3376 of title 5.

children.heading: Authority to accept Federal detailees
children.label: 7 Authority to accept Federal detailees
children.text: The Director may accept officers or employees of the United States or members of the uniformed service on a detail from an element of the Federal Government, on a reimbursable or a nonreimbursable basis, as jointly agreed to by the heads of the receiving and detailing elements, for a period not to exceed 3 years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director shall appoint program managers for 3-year terms (and may reappoint such program managers for 1 additional consecutive 3-year term) for the programs carried out by ARPA–H.

children.label: A
children.text: establish, in consultation with the Director, research and development goals for programs, including timelines and milestones, and make such goals available to the public;

children.label: B
children.text: manage applications and proposals, through the appropriate officials, for making awards as described in subsection (g) for activities consistent with the goals and functions described in subsection (b);

children.label: C
children.text: issue funding opportunity announcements, using uniform administrative processes, as appropriate;

children.label: i
children.text: the scientific, technical merit, and novelty of the proposed project;

children.label: ii
children.text: the ability of the applicant to successfully carry out the proposed project;

children.label: iii
children.text: the potential future commercial applications of the project proposed by the applicant, including whether such applications may have the potential to address areas of currently unmet need within biomedicine and improve health outcomes;

children.label: iv
children.text: the degree to which the proposed project has the potential to transform biomedicine and addresses a scientific or technical question pursuant to subsection (b);

children.label: v
children.text: the potential for the project to take an interdisciplinary approach; and

children.label: vi
children.text: such other criteria as established by the Director;

children.label: E
children.text: provide project oversight and management of strategic initiatives to advance the program, including by conducting project reviews not later than 18 months after the date of funding awards to identify and monitor progress of milestones with respect to each project and prior to disbursement of additional funds;

children.label: F
children.text: provide recommendations to the Director with respect to advancing the goals and functions specified in subsection (b);

children.label: G
children.text: encourage research collaborations and cultivate opportunities for the application or utilization of successful projects, including through identifying and supporting applicable public-private partnerships or partnerships between or among award recipients;

children.label: H
children.text: provide recommendations to the Director to establish, expand, restructure, or terminate partnerships or projects; and

children.label: I
children.text: communicate and collaborate with leaders and experts within the health care and biomedical research and development fields, including from both the public and private sectors and, as necessary, through the convening of workshops and meetings, to identify research and development gaps and opportunities and solicit stakeholder input on programs and goals.

children.label: i
children.text: a description of projects supported by ARPA–H in the previous fiscal year and whether such projects are meeting the goals developed by the Director pursuant to subsection (c)(4)(A);

children.label: ii
children.text: a description of projects terminated in the previous fiscal year, and the reason for such termination;

children.label: iii
children.text: a description of planned programs starting in the next fiscal year, pending the availability of funding;

children.label: iv
children.text: activities conducted in coordination with other Federal departments and agencies;

children.label: v
children.text: a description of any successes with, or barriers to, coordinating with other Federal departments and agencies to achieve the goals and functions under subsection (b);

children.label: vi
children.text: aggregated demographic information, if available, of direct recipients and performers in funded projects and of the ARPA–H workforce (consistent with the reporting requirements under paragraph (3)); and

children.label: vii
children.text: a summary of award recipient compliance with .section 242v of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the Committee on Energy and Commerce and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 5 years after , the Director shall seek to enter into an agreement with the National Academies under which the National Academies conducts an evaluation of whether ARPA–H is meeting the goals and functions specified in subsection (b).December 29, 2022

children.heading: Submission of results
children.label: B Submission of results
children.text: The agreement entered into under subparagraph (A) shall require the National Academies to submit the evaluation conducted under such agreement to the Director, the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate, and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives, and make the report publicly available.

children.label: i
children.text: the number of positions filled through such authority;

children.label: ii
children.text: the types of appointments of such positions;

children.label: iii
children.text: the titles, occupational series, and grades of such positions;

children.label: iv
children.text: the number of positions publicly noticed to be filled under such authority;

children.label: v
children.text: the number of qualified applicants who apply for such positions;

children.label: vi
children.text: the qualification criteria for such positions; and

children.label: vii
children.text: the demographic information of individuals appointed to such positions.

children.heading: Reports to Congress
children.label: B Reports to Congress
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , and annually thereafter for each fiscal year in which such authority is used, the Director shall submit to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives a report describing the total number of appointments filled under subsection (i) within the fiscal year and how the positions relate to the goals and functions of ARPA–H.December 29, 2022

children.label: i
children.text: the number of positions publicly noticed and filled under the authority under subsection (i);

children.label: ii
children.text: the occupational series, grades, and types of appointments of such positions;

children.label: iii
children.text: how such positions related to advancing the goals and functions of ARPA–H;

children.label: iv
children.text: how the Director made appointment decisions under subsection (i);

children.label: v
children.text: a summary of sources used to identify candidates for filling such positions, as applicable;

children.label: vi
children.text: the number of individuals appointed;

children.label: vii
children.text: aggregated demographic information related to individuals appointed; and

children.label: viii
children.text: any challenges, limitations, or gaps related to the use of the authority under subsection (i) and any related recommendations to address such challenges, limitations, or gaps.

children.heading: Strategic plan
children.label: l Strategic plan
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , and every 3 years thereafter, the Director shall provide to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives a strategic plan describing how ARPA–H will carry out investments each fiscal year in the following 3-year period. The requirements regarding individual institute and center strategic plans under , including paragraph (3) of such subsection, shall not apply to ARPA–H.December 29, 2022section 282(m) of this title
to assess the degree of any potential duplication of existing Federal programs and projects; and
to make any recommendations regarding any potential reorganization, consolidation, or termination of such programs and projects.

children.label: A
children.text: prioritize awarding grants, cooperative agreements, contracts, prizes, and other transaction awards to entities that will conduct funded work in the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: as appropriate and practicable, encourage nondomestic recipients of any grants, cooperative agreements, contracts, prizes, and other transactions under this section to collaborate with a domestic entity;

children.label: C
children.text: not make awards under this section to nondomestic entities organized under the laws of a covered foreign country (as defined in ); andsection 3059 of title 50

children.label: D
children.text: in accordance with the requirements of chapter 33 of title 41 and the Federal Acquisition Regulation, not make awards under this section to entities that have more than 3 ongoing concurrent awards under this section.

children.heading: Clarification
children.label: 2 Clarification
children.text: In making an award under this section, the Director may waive the requirements of subparagraphs (A), (B), and (D) of paragraph (1) if such requirements cannot reasonably be met, and the proposed project has the potential to advance the goals described in subsection (b)(1). The Director shall provide notice to Congress not later than 30 days after waiving such requirements.
the President’s Council of Advisors on Science and Technology;
representatives of professional or scientific organizations, including academia and industry, with expertise in specific technologies under consideration or development by ARPA–H;
an existing advisory committee providing advice to the Secretary or the head of any operating or staff division of the Department;
the advisory committee established under subsection (p); and
any other entity the Director may deem appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There is established an ARPA–H Interagency Advisory Committee (referred to in this subsection as the “Advisory Committee”) to coordinate efforts and provide advice and assistance on specific program or project tasks and the overall direction of ARPA–H.

children.label: A
children.text: The National Institutes of Health.

children.label: B
children.text: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.

children.label: C
children.text: The Food and Drug Administration.

children.label: D
children.text: The Office of the Assistant Secretary for Preparedness and Response.

children.label: E
children.text: The Office of the Assistant Secretary of Health.

children.label: F
children.text: The Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency.

children.label: G
children.text: The Office of Science of the Department of Energy.

children.label: H
children.text: The National Science Foundation.

children.label: I
children.text: Any other agency or office with subject matter expertise that the Director of ARPA–H determines appropriate to advance programs or projects under this section.

children.heading: Nonapplicability of FACA
children.label: 3 Nonapplicability of FACA
children.text: The Federal Advisory Committee Act (5 U.S.C. App.) shall not apply to the Advisory Committee.2See References in Text note below.2

children.heading: Advisory nature
children.label: 4 Advisory nature
children.text: The functions of the Advisory Committee shall be advisory in nature, and nothing in this subsection shall be construed as granting such Committee authority over the activities authorized under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Director, in consultation with the Advisory Committee, shall develop a performance measures framework for programs or projects supported by ARPA–H in order to inform and facilitate the evaluation required under subsection (k)(2), including identification of any data needed to perform such evaluation,

children.heading: Availability of performance measures
children.label: B Availability of performance measures
children.text: The Director shall provide to the National Academies such performance measures and data necessary to perform the evaluation required under subsection (k)(2).

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: q Rule of construction
children.text: The authorities under this section, with respect to the Director, are additional authorities that do not supersede or modify any existing authorities.

that has potential to revolutionize the detection, diagnosis, mitigation, prevention, cure, or treatment of a disease or health condition that can cause severe health outcomes and which is an area of currently unmet need; and

children.label: A
children.text: significant scientific or technical challenges exist; or

children.label: B
children.text: incentives in the commercial market are unlikely to result in the adequate or timely development of such capability or technology.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: s Authorization of appropriations
children.text: To carry out this section, there is authorized to be appropriated $500,000,000 for each of the fiscal years 2024 through 2028, to remain available until expended.

children.heading: Additional budget clarification
children.label: t Additional budget clarification
children.text: Any budget request for ARPA–H shall propose a separate appropriation from the other accounts of the National Institutes of Health.
